flags.concern: Step 6 is aimed at the wrong implementation gap: the timeline already has drag-over boundary styling, but external drops still cannot target a new track below the list because the drop math and drop handler only resolve existing rows.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css:65` already defines `data-drag-over` styling and `:69` already defines the `drop-target-new-track` affordance. The real limitation is in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts:243`, which clamps `rowIndex` to `rowCount - 1`, and in `:643-646` plus `:734-738`, which only resolve compatible existing tracks.

flags.concern: Step 4 does not cover the existing same-row move indicator in `TimelineEditor`, so the stated internal/external drag consistency goal would still be only partially addressed.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:45-50`, `:146-185`, and `:216-245` render a separate `dragIndicator` overlay for normal action moves. Its styling lives in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css:32-63`. Step 4 only mentions `useTimelineEditing.ts` and `useCrossTrackDrag.ts`.

flags.concern: Step 2 hides tabs but never clamps or resets `activeTab`, which can leave the properties panel on an invisible tab after switching clip types.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:94` binds `Tabs` directly to `activeTab`, and `:95-100` currently renders all triggers. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx:28-33` only auto-switches into `text` for text clips; there is no inverse reset when the selected clip is media or audio.

flags.concern: Step 3 understates the data dependency for opening a source lightbox from the preview: `clipMeta.asset` alone is not enough, and many assets have no generation-backed lightbox available.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/AssetPanel.tsx:148-186` builds lightbox queries from `registry[assetKey].generationId`, and `:267-303` only enables the source button when generation data is actually loaded. `OverlayEditor` currently only has clip meta plus the asset registry entry (`/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:8-20`), not the loaded `GenerationRow` that `MediaLightbox` needs.

flags.concern: Scope creep: Step 8 turns one bug in this bundle into an open-ended render-pipeline project, but the repo currently exposes only a toast stub and no video-editor render backend contract.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts:603-606` is an explicit toast-only `startRender` stub. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/data/DataProvider.ts:19-30` has no render method, and `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:11-149` implements timeline/asset loading and uploads only. Repo search also found no existing video-editor render task type or worker wiring to support options A or B.
